---
title: "Paper Title"
subtitle: "Journal, Year | Presenter Name | YYYY-MM-DD"
author: "SNU MSE Computational Science Journal Club"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, styles.scss]
    width: 1600
    height: 900
    margin: 0.06
    transition: none
    slide-number: c/t
    footer: "SNU MSE Computational Science Journal Club"
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Paper snapshot

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="58%"}
**Citation**

- Authors:
- Journal / year:
- DOI:
- Field: DFT / MLIP / AI / LLM / other

**One-line summary**

> Replace this with the single most important message of the paper.
:::

::: {.column width="42%"}
![](assets/paper_figure_overview.png){fig-alt="Representative figure from the paper"}

::: {.caption}
From/Adapted from Author et al.
:::
:::
::::

::: {.notes}
Start by telling the audience what kind of paper this is and why it is worth discussing.
:::

## This paper addresses a specific bottleneck in [field/problem]

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Known context**

- What is already known?
- What materials system or task matters here?
- Why does this problem matter for materials science?
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Open bottleneck**

- What remains hard?
- What did previous studies miss?
- Why is this paper timely?
:::
::::

## Previous approaches leave [gap] unresolved

Use this slide only if the paper needs context from earlier work.

| Approach | Strength | Remaining issue |
|---|---|---|
| Prior work 1 |  |  |
| Prior work 2 |  |  |
| This paper |  |  |

## The main claim is [claim in one sentence]

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="45%"}
**Authors' claim**

- Claim 1:
- Claim 2:
- Claim 3:
:::

::: {.column width="55%"}
**Evidence map**

1. Method / dataset establishes ...
2. Key result 1 shows ...
3. Key result 2 supports ...
4. Validation confirms ...
:::
::::

# Method

## The study combines [method/data/model] to test the claim

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="52%"}
![](assets/method_pipeline.png){fig-alt="Method or workflow diagram"}

::: {.caption}
From/Adapted from Author et al.
:::
:::

::: {.column width="48%"}
**Method overview**

- Input:
- Calculation / model:
- Output:
- Validation:

**What to watch**

- Key assumption:
- Possible weak point:
:::
::::

## Computational details define the reliability boundary

Choose the relevant block and delete the others.

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="33%"}
**DFT**

- Code:
- XC functional:
- Cutoff / k-points:
- Supercell:
- U / vdW / SOC:
- Convergence:
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
**MLIP**

- DFT dataset:
- Model:
- Train/val/test:
- Active learning:
- Error metrics:
- OOD test:
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
**AI / LLM**

- Task:
- Dataset:
- Model:
- Prompt/RAG/tuning:
- Baselines:
- Metrics:
:::
::::

# Results

## Key result 1: [figure-level conclusion]

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="62%"}
![](assets/key_result_1.png){fig-alt="Key result figure 1"}

::: {.caption}
From/Adapted from Author et al.
:::
:::

::: {.column width="38%"}
**What was done**

- 

**What was observed**

- 

**Why it matters**

- 
:::
::::

## Key result 2: [figure-level conclusion]

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="62%"}
![](assets/key_result_2.png){fig-alt="Key result figure 2"}

::: {.caption}
From/Adapted from Author et al.
:::
:::

::: {.column width="38%"}
**What was done**

- 

**What was observed**

- 

**Why it matters**

- 
:::
::::

## Key result 3: [figure-level conclusion]

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="62%"}
![](assets/key_result_3.png){fig-alt="Key result figure 3"}

::: {.caption}
From/Adapted from Author et al.
:::
:::

::: {.column width="38%"}
**What was done**

- 

**What was observed**

- 

**Why it matters**

- 
:::
::::

## The evidence supports [main claim] through [mechanism/validation]

Use this slide to connect multiple figures.

| Evidence | Supports | Confidence |
|---|---|---|
| Figure 1 |  | High / Medium / Low |
| Figure 2 |  | High / Medium / Low |
| Figure 3 |  | High / Medium / Low |

# Critique

## The paper is strongest where [strength]

- Strong point 1:
- Strong point 2:
- Strong point 3:

## The main uncertainty is [limitation]

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Limitations**

- Data / structure coverage:
- Method assumption:
- Missing comparison:
- Generalizability:
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**What would make it stronger**

- Additional calculation:
- Additional benchmark:
- Ablation / control:
- Experimental validation:
:::
::::

## Discussion questions

1. Which evidence is most convincing, and why?
2. Which assumption is most fragile?
3. What result would change our interpretation of this paper?

## Take-home message

**One sentence**

> Replace this with the final message people should remember after the discussion.

**Three points**

- 
- 
- 

# Backup

## Backup: extra computational settings

Add full settings, hyperparameters, convergence tests, prompt details, or dataset information here.

## Backup: additional figures

Use backup slides for figures that are useful for questions but not essential to the main story.
